publisher: World Bank / PPIAF / Power Dialogue (70th Edition)

entries.policy_claim: Universal access as a goal via Nigeria Electrification Project and Rural Electrification Strategy
entries.consumer_reality: 80 million Nigerians without electricity as of 2022
programmes_referenced: Rural Electrification Strategy and Implementation Plan
suggested_ai_prompts: Why are 80 million Nigerians still without electricity?
What is the Nigeria Electrification Project and has it worked?

entries.policy_claim: Private sector participation post-2016 privatisation expected to improve supply
entries.consumer_reality: Average daily supply still below 4GW six years after privatisation — no measurable improvement for consumers
suggested_ai_prompts: Why did privatisation in 2016 not improve my power supply?
Who owns the electricity distribution companies in Nigeria?

entries.policy_claim: Presidential Power Initiative (PPI) with Siemens to deliver 7GW in Phase 1, scaling to 25GW by 2025
entries.consumer_reality: Average daily supply below 4GW at time of report (June 2022); 25GW target by 2025 not achieved
suggested_ai_prompts: What is the Presidential Power Initiative and did it deliver?
Why is Nigeria's electricity generation still so low despite government promises?

entries.policy_claim: Power Sector Recovery Programme (PSRP) to achieve financial sustainability and consistent tariff policy
entries.consumer_reality: Inconsistent tariff policy implementation documented as an ongoing structural problem
suggested_ai_prompts: What is PSRP and how does it affect my electricity bill?
Why does my electricity bill keep changing without better service?

entries.policy_claim: Reforms under PSRP to reduce network losses and improve financial sustainability
entries.consumer_reality: High, unsustainable losses in distribution network documented; costs effectively passed to consumers through tariffs
suggested_ai_prompts: Why do I pay for electricity losses I didn't cause?
What are distribution network losses and why do they affect my bill?

entries.policy_claim: USD 550 million from World Bank and AfDB via REA to connect rural communities at scale through mini-grids and solar home systems
entries.consumer_reality: Only 11,000 people connected through off-grid solutions — approximately 0.01% of the 80 million without access
suggested_ai_prompts: Has the Nigeria Electrification Project actually connected people to power?
What is Solar Power Naija and who does it reach?

entries.policy_claim: 14 solar projects to generate approximately 1GW of clean energy for Nigerian consumers
entries.consumer_reality: Projects explicitly kept off-grid because the national grid cannot absorb them; consumers cannot access clean energy output
suggested_ai_prompts: Why can't solar projects connect to the national grid?
What is Nigeria's Energy Transition Plan and does it help ordinary consumers?

entries.policy_claim: NTEP1 (Nigeria Transmission Expansion Programme Phase 1) to rehabilitate and upgrade transmission substations and lines across NW, SE, South-South regions
entries.consumer_reality: Repeated vandalism of transmission towers (6 destroyed in Delta alone) causing blackouts; framed as a coordination problem rather than a consumer protection failure
suggested_ai_prompts: Why does vandalism keep causing blackouts and what is being done?
What is NTEP1 and has it improved transmission in my region?

entries.policy_claim: Government and World Bank to create enabling environment for private investment to meet universal energy access targets
entries.consumer_reality: Government and development partners explicitly acknowledge they cannot meet financing requirements alone; universal access remains contingent on private sector participation that has not materialised at scale
suggested_ai_prompts: Why is Nigeria's electricity problem still not solved despite billions in investment?
What role does the private sector play in Nigeria's electricity supply?

meta.key_stat: 80 million Nigerians without electricity access as of June 2022
meta.most_damning_finding: USD 550 million deployed for off-grid access connected only 11,000 people
meta.policy_ambition_vs_reality_headline: PPI promised 25GW by 2025; actual supply was below 4GW at report date
